data.title: Novo Nordisk teams up with OpenAI to boost AI-driven drug discovery efforts
data.description:
Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk (NVO) on Tuesday announced a strategic partnership with OpenAI (OPENAI) aimed at accelerating its AI transformation in healthcare and speeding up the development of new treatment options for patients.

The partnership will apply advanced AI capabilities to analyse complex datasets, identify promising drug candidates, and reduce the time required to move from research to patient. It has been structured with strict data protection, governance, and human oversight to ensure ethical and compliant use, the companies said.

“Integrating AI in our everyday work gives us the ability to analyse datasets at a scale that was previously impossible, identify patterns we could not see, and test hypotheses faster than ever,” said Novo Nordisk (NVO) CEO Mike Doustdar.

OpenAI will assist Novo Nordisk in upskilling the company’s global workforce and enhancing AI literacy. The partnership will also apply OpenAI’s capabilities to improve efficiency in manufacturing, supply chain and distribution, and corporate operations.

Pilot programmes will launch across research & development, manufacturing, and commercial operations, with full integration by the end of 2026.

“AI is reshaping industries and in life sciences, it can help people live better, longer lives,” said Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI (OPENAI). “This collaboration with Novo Nordisk will help them accelerate scientific discovery, run smarter global operations, and redefine the future of patient care.”

data.title: Publicis Groupe S.A. reports Q1 results; reaffirms FY outlook
data.description:
• Publicis Groupe S.A. press release (PGPEF): Q1
  • revenue organic growth of +6.4% leading to net revenue organic growth of +4.5%, in line with expectations.
  • Revenue of €3.46B.
  • Reaffirming FY’26 guidance:
    • Net revenue organic growth of +4% to +5%, with rock solid floor
    • Slight operating margin improvement vs. industry-high rate of 18.2% in FY’25
    • Free cash flow at c. €2.1 billion

data.title: China is said to relax curbs on some BHP iron ore cargoes
data.description:
• China’s state-backed iron ore buyer has told several steel mills in the country they are allowed to purchase some BHP Group (BHP) cargoes, an apparent concession in a months-long commercial dispute, Bloomberg News reported.
  • Officials from China Mineral Resources Group Co. told plants they can resume bidding for some BHP cargoes denominated in US dollars from Tuesday, according to people familiar with the matter.
  • Mills have not yet been explicitly allowed to take BHP (BHP) physical deliveries from Chinese ports, but a notice is expected, they said, asking not to be named discussing private commercial details.

data.title: Blue Owl private credit fund raises $400M via bond market
data.description:
Blue Owl Capital's private credit fund, through its listed vehicle Blue Owl Capital Corp. (OBDC), raised $400M via a five-year bond offering, marking its first deal in over a month.

The investment-grade rated notes mature in 2028 and yield about 6.5%. The OBDC (OBDC) bond sale was priced at a roughly 2.7 percentage point spread to similar-maturity Treasuries. 

The proceeds from these notes will be used to pay down certain existing indebtedness, its SEC filing showed.

The deal comes at a sensitive time for Blue Owl and the broader private credit industry, which has faced rising redemption requests, valuation scrutiny, and tighter financing conditions in recent months.

U.S. BDC spreads have widened in recent weeks, rising to roughly 2.25 percentage points over their benchmark, up from about 1.7 percentage points in January, a Bloomberg report pointed.

data.title: FRP Holdings reports Q4 results
data.description:
• FRP Holdings press release (FRPH): Q4 77% decrease in net income ($0.4 million vs $1.7 million) due to expenses related to the Altman Logistics platform acquisition ($0.5 million).
  • Revenue of $10.92M (+3.7% Y/Y).

  • Net operating income increased slightly ($9.29 million vs $9.10 million).

data.title: Viasat enters Boeing evaluation process for next-gen AERA antenna terminal
data.description:
• Viasat (VSAT) has entered Boeing’s (BA) technical evaluation process to assess and qualify Viasat AERA, the company’s next-generation electronically steered antenna terminal, across all current Boeing commercial airplane programs. 
  • Following certification of the system, Viasat AERA will be a selectable linefit option, enabling airlines to incorporate Viasat’s next-generation, innovative hardware -- which is part of its widely adopted Viasat AMARA solution -- directly into their aircraft configuration and future fleet planning, the company said.
  • “This agreement continues our long tradition of working to bring powerful connectivity solutions to the market and is an important milestone in bringing Viasat AERA to airlines around the world,” said Brian Simone, vice president of aviation products, Viasat (VSAT). 
  • Also at the Aircraft Interior Expo, Viasat demonstrated progress on readiness for its planned entry of the Viasat AERA terminal into the market in early 2028, outlining innovative technologies and capabilities that will enable both airline and passenger benefits.

data.title: Solaris Energy files for 4.18M stock offering by selling shareholders
data.description:
• Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) announced a secondary offering of 4,182,772 shares by current stockholders; notably, the firm will not see any capital from this transaction.
  • The stock was up 1.5% in extended trading.

data.title: Orla Mining stays on track to meet FY gold production guidance
data.description:
• Orla Mining (ORLA) remains on track to meet its full-year consolidated gold production guidance range of 340,000–360,000 ounces, it said late on Monday, adding that total gold production reached 81,206 oz, while gold sales came in slightly higher at 81,540 oz in Q1 2026.
  • "2026 was always going to be a catalyst-rich year, and the opening months have not disappointed," CEO Jason Simpson said. 
  • During the quarter, Musselwhite mined 333,495 tonnes of ore and processed 332,822 tonnes at a mill head grade of 6.29 g/t gold. Gold recovery was 95.91%, resulting in gold production of 62,985 ounces. Musselwhite completed 3,437 meters of lateral development. 367 meters of the completed lateral development was attributed to the advance of the 1080 exploration drift. 
  • At March 31, 2026, Orla's (ORLA) cash and debt positions were $427.3 million and $331.3 million, respectively, resulting in a net cash position of $96.0 million.

data.title: Tajiri Resources adds C$2M concurrent offering to ongoing financing
Cardinal Energy declares CAD 0.06 dividend
data.description:
• Cardinal Energy (CRLFF) declares CAD 0.06/share monthly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 6.37%
  • Payable May 15; for shareholders of record April 30; ex-div April 30.
  • See CRLFF Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
